title: Address and Dereference
instruction: &x gives the memory address of x. *p dereferences a pointer to get the value. Declare x := 42, store its address in p, then print the value via *p.

title: Modify via Pointer
instruction: Define double(p *int) that multiplies the value at p by 2. Call it with &x where x is 42, then print x (should be 84).

title: Pointer to Struct
instruction: Create a pointer to a Person (with Name field), update the name to Alice through the pointer, then print the name.

title: Swap with Pointers
instruction: Implement swap(a, b *int) that swaps two integers. Start with x=10 and y=20, call swap, then print x and y (20, 10).
